# Backend (stack-agnostic)

_Applies when: building or modifying any backend / API service in this org, regardless of stack._

Cross-cutting backend truths shared by every backend stack (Node, Kotlin, …). Concrete
per-stack tooling lives in the stack-specific rule — `05-stack.md` for the Node/TS stack,
`08-stack-backend-kotlin.md` for the Kotlin/JVM stack.

## Contracts & release order

- Contract-first APIs: define request/response shapes as shared schemas (the single source
  of truth) and expose an auto-generated OpenAPI document from them — clients derive from the
  contract, never hand-copy shapes across services.
- When a change spans dependent packages, ship in dependency order:
  **shared-types/contracts → api → web/mobile** — never in parallel. Shared contract types
  are defined first, the API next, then the clients.

## Data access & secrets

- Data-access source of truth: enforce access rules at the data layer, not only in the app.
  With Supabase, RLS policies are the source of truth for who can read/write.
- No secrets in code or config — reference credentials via environment variables.
